The Planet

Every The Planet story we're smiling about, freshest first.

⚡ The Planet

For the First Time Ever, the World Made More Electricity From Wind and Sun Than Gas

In April 2026, wind and solar produced 531 terawatt-hours of power worldwide — overtaking gas for the first time.

via Ember · 6h

🐳 Animals

North Atlantic Right Whales Just Had Their Best Baby Season Since 2009

Twenty-three calves were spotted this season — a hopeful sign for one of the world's most endangered whales.

via NOAA Fisheries · 11h

🌍 The Planet

via Positive News · 12h

🪶 Science Wins

From 27 Birds to More Than 500: The California Condor's Long Climb Back From the Brink

In 1987 every last condor was in captivity. Today over half of them fly free.

via Earth.com · 16h

🦅 The Planet

Back From the Brink: America's Bald Eagle Soars From 500 Pairs to 14,000

Once down to fewer than 500 breeding pairs, the national bird has rebounded to roughly 14,000.

via U.S. Dept. of the Interior · 22h

🏞️ The Planet

Sweden Tore Down Three Dams and Set 100 Kilometers of River Free

With the dams gone, the Vindel River is flowing wild again — and crayfish and trout are already coming back.

via Rewilding Europe · 1d

How Cloudbreak works

We comb publishers across the web for the stories that make your day a little brighter, show you the headline and a quick summary, then send you straight to the original source for the full story. We don't republish anyone's work — good news belongs to everyone, and credit belongs to the people who reported it.

Spotted something good?

A neighbor, a rescue, a small win on your street — if it made you smile, send it our way.

Send us a tip →
